name: Muhammad al-Awsat
title: Son of Ali
shortDescription: Another son of Amir al-Mu'minin Ali (AS) who was martyred on Ashura after a manifest battle defending the Master of Martyrs.
fullStory:
Muhammad ibn Ali ibn Abi Talib, known as Muhammad al-Awsat (the middle Muhammad), was one of the brave sons of Amir al-Mu'minin (AS). His mother was Umamah bint Abi al-As, a descendant of the Prophet (PBUH).

Muhammad was raised in a family that considered death in the path of God an honor. When his brother Imam Hussain (AS) decided to rise up, Muhammad joined him to assist the religion of his grandfather.

On the day of Ashura, with the start of the Hashemite youths' battle, Muhammad al-Awsat also rushed to the field. He struck with his sword with Haidari firmness and bravery, and through his epic poetry, made his name and noble lineage known to the disloyal Kufans.

After a long battle and taking down a number of Umar Sa'd's soldiers, he was surrounded by someone from the Bani Darim tribe and was martyred by spear and sword strikes, immortalizing his name among the devotees of Wilayah.
quote: (From Ziyarat Nahiya:) Peace be upon Muhammad, son of Amir al-Mu'minin.
